summary_one_line: Input must carry the 4-byte 'Luv ' colorspace signature at offset 0x10 so the switch dispatches to case cmsSigLuvData.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte big-endian FOURCC at input offset 0x10 must equal the cmsSigLuvData signature 'Luv ' (4c 75 76 20). _cmsLCMScolorSpace switches on this parsed colorspace signature; only that exact value drives the switch to case cmsSigLuvData (line 819, the blocked true side).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry 4c 75 76 ('Luv') at offsets 0x10-0x12, exercising line 819; the byte-diff shows offset 0x10 is 4c(L)x8, 0x11 75(u)x6, 0x12 76(v)x6 on the W side, and W's T-count on line 819 is non-zero (T=11).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry 52 47 42 ('RGB') at offsets 0x10-0x12, dispatching to case cmsSigRgbData (line 815) and never reaching line 819; the loser's true-count on the blocker line is zero (T=0 F=56).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace: cmplog logs the 32-bit comparison operand cmsSigLuvData seen inside the switch and splices it back into the input at the offset that fed the compare, planting 'Luv ' exactly. value_profile (loser) lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets a CMP_MAP Hamming-distance bucket, which cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte FOURCC against an equality dispatch, so it stalls on RGB.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is case cmsSigLuvData at line 819, the globally blocked true side, inside a switch on the parsed colorspace signature.
The enclosing function switches on the profile colorspace value, with case cmsSigLuvData the W-only resolved side and the RGB case hit by both.
Winner seeds carry the ASCII 'Luv ' signature at offset 0x10 while losers carry 'RGB'.
A winner-resolving seed contains the literal bytes 'Luv XYZ' starting at offset 0x10.
A loser-blocking seed contains 'RGB XYZ' at offset 0x10 and never takes the cmsSigLuvData side.
The loser never takes the true side of the blocker; the winner does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 819  T=11 F=18  T=0 F=56  case cmsSigLuvData:  return  PT_YUV;

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog (winner) has I2SRandReplace whereas value_profile (loser) does not.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and relies on CMP_MAP distance buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2248 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed d82c60bf4a9baa76` and observed an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 4 of the ancestor chain (tool reported '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 4'), the ParentInfo-only signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current vpc build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same 4-byte equality-switch FOURCC dispatch in which value_profile resolves the cmsSigLuvData side as often as value_profile_cmplog (no I2S advantage), showing CMP_MAP distance feedback alone suffices to plant the exact signature.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset that feeds the switch comparand is inferred from the byte-diff/seed layout (0x10) rather than confirmed by a logged compare-to-offset mapping, so the dataflow from byte 0x10 into the switch is assumed.
